title: Accessing hardware resources in distributed computing environments
abstract: Systems, methods, and apparatuses are described herein for accessing hardware resources in distributed computing environments, Remote hardware (e.g,, hardware resources) may be accessed, for example, using interfaces (e.g., device driver interfaces). Remote hardware (e.g,, within a terminal and/or on the edge) may be accessed, for example, to enable computationally intensive applications (e.g., multi-player games, augmented/virtual reality) to be run on terminal devices, A wireless transmit/receive unit (WTRU) may enable remote hardware access.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What Is Claimed Is: 1. A wireless transmit/receive unit (WTRU), the WTRU comprising: a processor, the processor configured to: receive a first message from a network, wherein the first message indicates a list of hardware resource identifiers for one or more hardware resources; send a second message to the network, wherein the second message indicates a request to establish a data session, and wherein the second message indicates a resource identifier for a hardware resource selected from the list of hardware resource identifiers; and receive a third message from the network, wherein the third message indicates configuration information to access the selected hardware resource, wherein the configuration information is associated with a first server that provides the hardware resource, and wherein the third message is received in response to the request to establish the data session. 2. The WTRU of claim 1 , wherein the processor is further configured to send a fourth message using the data session, wherein the fourth message comprises data for the selected hardware resource, 3. The WTRU of claim 1 or 2, wherein the processor is further configured to receive a fifth message, wherein the fifth message indicates a second server associated with the resource identifier indicated in the second message. 4. The WTRU of claim 3, wherein the second server is an edge configuration server. 5. The WTRU of any of claims 1 to 4, wherein the configuration information Indicates at least, one of a configuration identification, a reference to an access token, a reference to a broker object, a handle associated with a time stamp counter, a flags register, a reference to a buffer, an access to a call, an access to a fault code, or an access to an operation. 6. The WTRU of any of claims 1 to 5. wherein the request sent to the network indicates a user intent, wherein the user intent is mapped to the selected hardware resource. 7. The WTRU of any of claims 1 to 6, wherein the processor is further configured to send initialization information to the network, wherein the initialization information indicates at least one of the location of the WTRU, a WTRU identification, or a hardware criteria tor an application executing on the WTRU. 8. The WTRU of any of claims 1 to 7, wherein the location of the WTRU is associated with at least one of a geographical coordinate, a network location, a distance from the WTRU, a distance within a network, or a number of hops. 9. The WTRU of any of claims 1 to 8, wherein the hardware criteria is at least one of a quality of service associated with the application, a quality of service associated with the data session, a performance requirement for the hardware resource, a memory requirement for the hardware resource, or a context associated with the application. 10. The WTRU of any of claims 1-9, wherein the hardware resource identifier is selected based on a context associated with the application, and wherein the context is at least one of a number of hops between the WTRU and the first sewer, a network condition, a processor utilization associated with the WTRU, a memory utilization associated with the WTRU, a number of players in a game, an image to be displayed, a color of a display background, a scenery, a number of objects available to a player, a viewing direction, a time of day, a location, or an audience rating. 11. A method comprising: receiving a first message from a network, wherein the first message indicates a list of hardware resource identifiers for one or more hardware resources; sending a second message to the network, wherein the second message indicates a request to establish a data session, and wherein the second message indicates a resource identifier for a hardware resource selected from the list of hardware resource identifiers; and receiving a third message from the network, wherein the third message indicates configuration information to access the selected hardware resource, wherein the configuration information is associated 

with a first server that provides the hardware resource, and wherein the third message is received in response to the request to establish the data session. 12. The method of claim 11, further comprising: sending a fourth message using the data session, wherein the fourth message comprises data for the selected hardware resource. 13. The method of claim 11 or 12, further comprising: receiving a fifth message, wherein the fifth message indicates a second server associated with the resource identifier indicated in the second message. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the second server is an edge configuration server. 15. The method of any of claims 11 to 14, wherein the configuration information indicates at least one of a configuration identification, a reference to an access token, a reference to a broker object, a handle associated with a time stamp counter, a flags register, a reference to a buffer, an access to a call, an access to a fault code, or an access to an operation. 16. The method of any of claims 11 to 15, wherein the request sent to the network indicates a user intent, wherein the user intent Is mapped to the selected hardware resource. 17. The method of any of claims 11 to 16, further comprising: sending initialization information to the network, wherein the initialization information indicates at least one of the location of a WTRU, a WTRU identification, or a hardware criteria for an application executing on the WTRU, 18. The method of any of claims 11 to 17, wherein a location of a WTRU is associated with at least one of a geographical coordinate, a network location, a distance from the WTRU, a distance within a network, or a number of hops, 19. The method of any of claims 11 to 18, wherein the hardware criteria is at least one of a quality of service associated with the application, a quality of service associated with the data session, a performance requirement for the hardware resource, a memory requirement tor the hardware resource, or a context associated with the application, 20. The method of any of claims 11 to 19, wherein the hardware resource identifier is selected based on a context associated with the application, and wherein the context is at least one of a number of hops between the WTRU and the first server, a network condition, a processor utilization associated with the WTRU, a memory utilization associated with the WTRU, a number of players in a game, an image to be displayed, a color of a display background, a scenery, a number of objects available to a player, a viewing direction, a time of day, a location, or an audience rating.
